summary: Design for adding is_terminal_state(state) -> bool to lib.py: a pure, additive helper that returns True for 'done', 'failed', 'blocked' and False for all other inputs including None and non-strings. Includes focused pytest unit tests in a new tests/test_lib_is_terminal_state.py file. No existing call sites are refactored.

ledger.what: lib.py exists at .github/agent-factory/engine/lib.py and has no existing is_terminal_state
ledger.why: Need to confirm the target file and that the function is truly absent before specifying its placement
ledger.what_i_did: Read lib.py and grep for is_terminal_state across the repo; confirmed: file exists at expected path, function absent, PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS is at line 586

blast_radius.why: No production impact if wrong; the build simply fails cleanly with no existing code changed

reversibility.why: No change to existing code if the function is not added

ledger.revisit_if: If the file is relocated in a future engine refactor

ledger.what: The complete set of terminal states is exactly {"done", "failed", "blocked"} and is stable
ledger.why: The helper must enumerate the right set; an incorrect or incomplete set gives callers wrong answers
ledger.what_i_did: Verified against PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS in lib.py (lines 586-591), which lists exactly these three keys alongside the non-terminal 'setup' key; confirmed by the issue's explicit enumeration

blast_radius.why: If the set were wrong, any future call site that adopts the helper would silently mis-classify states; immediate blast radius is limited to new tests since no call sites are refactored in this change

reversibility.why: The frozenset literal is a one-line change

ledger.revisit_if: If a new terminal state (e.g. 'abandoned') is added to the engine

ledger.what: Place _TERMINAL_STATES + is_terminal_state immediately after PHASE_LABEL_COLOR in lib.py
ledger.why: Multiple placement options; grouping with PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS is semantically coherent because both are about the same terminal/special state identifiers
ledger.what_i_did: Chose proximity to PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS (~line 592) as the clearest semantic home; this keeps related constants co-located

blast_radius.why: Placement has no behavioral effect; Python resolves imports regardless of order within a module

reversibility.why: Can move the function anytime without changing behavior

ledger.revisit_if: If lib.py is restructured into clearly delineated sections

ledger.what: Use 'state in frozenset(...)' without an explicit isinstance guard
ledger.why: The issue requires is_terminal_state(None) to return False without raising; Python's 'in' on a frozenset is safe for any hashable value
ledger.what_i_did: Chose the idiomatic 'state in _TERMINAL_STATES' pattern; added explicit test cases for None and an integer to lock in this behavior

blast_radius.why: Affects only this function's edge-case behavior; all acceptance criteria are covered by tests

reversibility.why: Can add an isinstance guard anytime without changing behavior for valid string inputs

ledger.revisit_if: If Python's frozenset membership semantics change (extremely unlikely)

ledger.what: New test file tests/test_lib_is_terminal_state.py rather than appending to test_engine.py or test_decide.py
ledger.why: A focused, self-contained module keeps test files thematically coherent; test_decide.py tests lib.decide(), test_engine.py tests CAS/planner; neither is the right home for a simple predicate helper
ledger.what_i_did: Chose a new file following test_decide.py's pattern (same sys.path.insert approach, no external fixtures needed)

blast_radius.why: Test organization has no production impact; the test file is a dev-only artifact

reversibility.why: Can merge into another file anytime

ledger.revisit_if: If the project adopts a single-file test convention for lib.py helpers
